message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task #4 ready for review, need 9/9">
Task #4 (HTMX empty state transition) committed and pushed (4e049aa). CI is running.

ALL REVIEWERS: Please review and send consent or concerns. We need 9/9 consensus.

Note: Carson pushed before receiving the process update — that's fine. The new flow (consensus before push) applies starting with Task #5.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me review the Task #4 changes.

content.text: Good. I've reviewed all the changes. Let me send my assessment.

input.content:
**Task #4 Review -- Heydon Pickering (Accessibility)** I consent. This is a well-considered approach to the empty state transition. Here's my analysis: **Key template change -- the empty message is now always in the DOM:** The old pattern was `{% if todos.is_empty() %}

No todos yet.

{% endif %}` -- the empty message was conditionally rendered server-side. That meant it didn't exist in the DOM when the list had items, so JS couldn't show it after the last deletion. The new pattern at `todos.html:39` is `

`. The element is always present, toggled with `hidden`. This is the correct approach for several reasons: 1. **`hidden` attribute is accessible** -- Elements with `hidden` are removed from the accessibility tree. Screen readers won't announce "No todos yet." when the list has items. When the last item is deleted and `hidden` is removed, the element becomes visible and discoverable. Clean. 2. **The `

    ` also uses `hidden` symmetrically** -- When the list is empty, `
      ` keeps the empty list from being announced ("list, 0 items" would be confusing). When items exist, the `
        ` is visible and the empty message is hidden. They toggle in opposition. A screen reader will always get the right context. 3. **Focus management integrates cleanly** -- From Task #3, when the last item is deleted, focus moves to `document.getElementById("title")` (the add input). The `updateEmptyState()` runs in the same `htmx:afterSettle` handler. So the user experience is: delete last item -> empty message appears -> focus lands on the add input. The user knows the list is empty and is positioned to add a new item. Logical flow. 4. **Add-to-empty-list transition** -- The `htmx:afterRequest` listener on the `.todo-add` form calls `updateEmptyState()` after a successful POST. When the first item is added, the `
          ` becomes visible (unhidden) and the empty message gets hidden. The live region announcement from the existing `HX-Trigger: announce` header will tell the screen reader user that the item was added. Good. 5. **Progressive enhancement intact** -- Without JS, the server renders the correct state on every page load: empty message shown when `todos.is_empty()`, list shown otherwise. The JS only handles the in-page transitions. 6. **No-JS baseline still works** -- The `hidden` attributes are set correctly in the server-rendered template. The `id="todo-empty"` is only used by JS; it doesn't affect the no-JS experience. No accessibility concerns. The `hidden` toggle pattern is clean and accessible.
input.summary: Task #4 a11y review: I consent, hidden toggle correct

content.text: Consent sent for Task #4. Standing by for Task #5 (HTMX inline editing), which will be the most complex from an accessibility perspective -- focus management on swap-in, keyboard operability of the inline form, and focus restoration on save/cancel.
